title: Plooz
content:

{{Quote|A ''Star Child'' has been sent to Endor! Surely this is a ''good'' omen that we should live in ''peace''!|Chief Chirpa|<ref name="e10" />}}
'''Plooz''' was an [[Alzarian/Legends|Alzarian]] [[Prince/Legends|prince]] and the son of [[King/Legends|King]] [[Gokus]]. While still very young, Plooz was effectively kidnapped by Gokus's [[General/Legends|general]], [[Sludd]], as part of an elaborate plot to seize control of the [[Planet/Legends|planets]] [[Alzar]] and [[Sooma]]. Plooz was taken to the latter world, where he met King [[Zornog]]. It appeared to Gokus that Zornog had kidnapped the child, which prompted the Alzarian king to ready a military invasion of Sooma. Meanwhile, Zornog had the [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomat]] [[Droid/Legends|droids]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] take Plooz back to his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]].

While in transit, their [[Starship/Legends|ship]] was attacked by Sludd, forcing R2-D2 to make a last ditch attempt to escape by jumping to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. However, Plooz, attempting to help, had disrupted the ship's [[Antimatter|anti-matter]] device, which created a [[Time travel/Legends|time warp]] in [[Realspace/Legends|space]] that caused the ship to travel into the future. Finding himself in the [[Endor system/Legends|Endor system]], Plooz panicked and jettisoned one of the craft's [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]]. Landing on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]], Plooz was hailed by the native [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]] as a '''"Star Child"''' that would bring good luck. He became embroiled in the failed [[Chirpa–Gorneesh peace summit|Chirpa-Gorneesh peace summit]] and was later retrieved by C-3PO and R2-D2. The trio returned back through the time warp to their own time, and they were able to foil Sludd's ''coup d'état'' in the process.

==Biography==
===Stowaway===
{{Quote|It's not ''my'' fault your son, ''Prince Plooz'', stowed away on a freighter and wound up on ''my'' planet!|King Zornog, of Sooma|<ref name="d4" />}}
Born to [[King/Legends|King]] [[Gokus]], [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] Plooz resided in the [[Alzarian palace]]. At some point in the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]], one of his father's [[General/Legends|generals]], [[Sludd]], had the child taken from the palace and placed on a [[Freighter/Legends|trade freighter]], bound for the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Sooma]]. Sludd, who was preparing to stage a ''coup d'état'' against Gokus and Sooma's King [[Zornog]], planned to make it appear as though the [[Sooman]] monarch had kidnapped Plooz. The general predicted that Gokus would react by sending a [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] against Sooma and starting a war between his world and theirs. In the chaos, Sludd planned to claim both worlds for himself.<ref name="d4" />

[[File:PloozStrangles.png|thumb|left|200px|Plooz inadvertently begins strangling King Zornog.]]

Upon arriving on Sooma, young Plooz made his way to into the [[Royal Palace (Sooma)|Sooman palace]], where he was met by King Zornog. The king kept the child by his side while communications were opened with [[Alzar]]. King Gokus was livid and began threatening the Sooman king. While he would not tolerate Gokus's bullying, Zornog insisted that Plooz had merely stowed away on a freighter, and that there was no kidnapping plot whatsoever. Gokus refused to accept Zornog's explanation, however, and demanded the return of Plooz within a day, or he would send his fleet to attack Sooma. Zornog had more immediate problems to deal with, as Plooz, hoping to play with the Sooman king, grasped the monarch's robes and began tugging, inadvertently throttling him.<ref name="d4" />

Two [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic couriers]], the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], who had recently arrived in the palace, were able to fend Plooz off. Grateful, Zornog gave the pair of droids his [[Sooman starship|fastest starship]], with which to ferry the young prince back to his father. While in transit, Plooz found his way into the [[Starship/Legends|ship's]] [[Food synthesizer/Legends|food synthesizer]], where he began devouring giant [[Neuvian sundae]]s, much to C-3PO's chagrin. However, as the ship continued on its course towards Alzar, battleships under the command of General Sludd bore down on the Sooman craft in attack formation. Panicking, C-3PO hailed the vessels, warning them that he had the [[Alzarian/Legends|Alzarian]] prince on board. Plooz watched on in horror as Sludd's visage materialized on the ship's viewscreen and informed the occupants that they would soon perish under his guns.<ref name="d4" />

===Lost in time===
[[File:PloozAntimatter.png|thumb|right|250px|Plooz disrupts the ship's anti-matter device.]]
{{Quote|<s></s>''WAAH!'' Me want to go ''home!''|Prince Plooz|<ref name="d4" />}}
As Sludd's craft swooped down on them, C-3PO had R2-D2 take evasive action and ordered him to prepare the craft's [[Antimatter|anti-matter]] system for [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]]. Plooz, overhearing the order, wanted to help and dashed for the anti-matter controls, tugging at the module's handles. In doing so, he disrupted the ship's anti-matter pods, preventing the proper use of the ship's hyperdrive. At the same time, Sludd's craft began firing [[electron torpedo]]s, causing C-3PO to hastily order his counterpart to jump to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] without the anti-matter system. The jump was made just before the torpedoes would have hit, placing Plooz, C-3PO and R2-D2 in relative safety.<ref name="d4" />

As they made the unorthodox hyperspace jump, the ship's inhabitants were thrown about the [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]]. While they regained composure, C-3PO noted, with great shock, that there were no [[Star/Legends|stars]] to be seen outside the ship, and the only thing visible was a bright, pink opening. As they flew through the opening, they could once again see stars, but R2-D2 pointed out that they had in fact traveled one hundred years into the future, and had created a [[Time travel/Legends|time warp]] by causing an anti-matter overload upon jumping to hyperspace.<ref name="d4" /> Though the [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid's]] calculations were inaccurate,<ref name="conf">The events of [[Star Wars: Ewoks (1985)|''Ewoks'']] occur within the same twenty years in between [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']] as the [[Star Wars: Droids (1986)|''Star Wars: Droids'']] series. The inconsistency was, however, repeated in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.</ref> the trio were in fact in the [[Endor system/Legends|Endor system]], in the [[Moddell sector/Legends|Moddell sector]]. Plooz wailed at the prospect and began pining for his native Alzar. He made a beeline for the ship's [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]], but was swiftly restrained by C-3PO. As the [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] turned his attention away from the Alzarian prince to give R2-D2 instructions, however, the child dashed for the pod again, this time successfully entering and jettisoning it.<ref name="d4" />

==="Star Child"===
{{Quote|Isn't the Star Child wonderful, Wicket?|Princess Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka|<ref name="d4" />}}
The pod entered the [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] of the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]], where it landed, abruptly, in a swamp near [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]]. A terrified Plooz emerged eventually, to the sight of a band of [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]]. The [[Chief/Legends|chief]] of Bright Tree Village, [[Chirpa/Legends|Chirpa]], had seen the pod make its fiery descent to Endor and had taken it as an omen. The village shaman, [[Logray/Legends|Logray]], had read in his [[fortune stones]] that a blinding flash of light, much like the one generated by the pod's fall, would bring good fortune to the Ewoks. Upon seeing Plooz, Chirpa hailed the Alzarian as a "Star Child," who had been sent to Endor from the stars as a sign that the upcoming [[Chirpa–Gorneesh peace summit|Chirpa-Gorneesh peace summit]] would be a success and would put an end to the [[Ewok–Dulok wars/Legends|Ewok–Dulok War]].<ref name="d4" />

Plooz, however, was less than enthusiastic and began crying loudly. He was pacified by [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket W. Warrick]], who offered him some [[Honey/Legends|honey]]. While consuming the Ewok victual, Plooz was picked up by Chirpa and taken back to Bright Tree Village, where he could be cared for by the settlement's inhabitants.<ref name="d4" /> As Chirpa and his [[Council of Elders (Ewok)/Legends|Council of Elders]] prepared to depart for their meeting with King [[Gorneesh/Legends|Gorneesh]] of the [[Dulok/Legends|Duloks]], Plooz hid away in a large honey pot, devouring the contents from within. He was eventually retrieved, bathed, and held aloft by Chirpa's entourage, as he was taken to the summit as a sign of peace.<ref name="e10" />

===Chirpa-Gorneesh peace summit===
[[File:PloozBreakdown.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Plooz is brought to the Chirpa-Gorneesh summit.]]
{{Quote|You ''go'' now Chirpa, or…there will be ''no more'' Star Baby!|King Gorneesh|<ref name="e10" />}}
The summit was held at a wooden bridge that crossed a deep chasm, with the two parties standing at opposite sides. Gorneesh summoned Chirpa, requesting that he cross the bridge and meet on the Dulok side. The chief did so, bringing Plooz along with him. Gorneesh concurred with Chirpa's description of the child as a good omen, but quickly snatched the child out of the Ewok's hands, declaring that Plooz was too good a sign to waste on the Ewoks. The Dulok king held the terrified Alzarian prince over the chasm, demanding that Chirpa and his retinue depart the now-failed summit immediately. Chirpa complied, but as he and his colleagues crossed back over the bridge, [[Umwak]], Gorneesh's [[Shaman/Legends|shaman]], cut one of the suspension ropes.<ref name="e10" />

At that moment, C-3PO, R2-D2, Warrick, and Chirpa's daughter, Princess [[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka]], approached the location of the summit, with the protocol droid demanding that Gorneesh unhand Plooz. The star [[Ibleam]]'s rays reflected off C-3PO's surface, making him glow and giving him the appearance of what the Duloks perceived to be an Ewok demon. Gorneesh, terrified, departed with his henchman, leaving Plooz behind. While Chirpa and his Elders were able to make it off the heavily damaged bridge, Plooz attempted to cross by himself, much to the concern of his friends and guardians. R2-D2 produced a rope that Warrick tied to himself in an attempt to go out and retrieve Plooz, but the bridge's ropes snapped quickly, leaving the young Ewok with little choice but to jump down into the chasm after the Alzarian prince. Warrick caught Plooz mid-fall and brought him back up to the top of the cliff. Overjoyed, Plooz ran to C-3PO, embracing the droid in relief.<ref name="e10" />

===Return===
[[File:PloozInTheLineOfFire.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Plooz shields C-3PO from Sludd.]]
{{Quote|<nowiki />''Must find…FATHER!''|Prince Plooz|<ref name="d4" />}}
The Ewoks escorted C-3PO, R2-D2 and Plooz back to the Sooman craft and bade them farewell. The trio reentered the time warp with little time to spare, as it closed just as they made their way through it. They returned to their own time intact, and although their time-traveling journey had taken hours, they came back mere minutes after their departure relative to their time. As such, they were once again in danger of being destroyed by Sludd's fleet, but C-3PO took charge, ordering R2-D2 to hurry towards Alzar. Their ship was struck by Sludd's fire and forced to land just near the palace.<ref name="e10" />

Plooz, instructed by C-3PO to bring his father to the ship, hurried through the corridors of the palace, eventually finding Gokus. He rushed back out to the droids, only to see them being held at gunpoint by Sludd himself. The Alzarian prince put himself in the line of fire by leaping onto C-3PO, while Gokus arrived and demanded an explanation from his general. None was needed, however, as R2-D2 played a recording of Sludd's earlier threat to kill Plooz and take over both Alzar and Sooma. Livid, Gokus had his general and his co-conspirators thrown in [[Prison/Legends|prison]]. Young Prince Plooz, however, could do little more than fall asleep.<ref name="e10" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|<s></s>''Me'' come!|Prince Plooz|<ref name="e10" />}}
The Alzarian Prince Plooz was a curious individual, constantly investigating technology and people. His curiosity would occasionally be detrimental, such as his inadvertent near-strangulation of King Zornog.<ref name="d4" /> Speaking in a fractured dialect, he displayed great affection for his father, as well as C-3PO and R2-D2.<ref name="e10" /> Plooz was also partial to sweet foods, like sundaes and honey. Despite his age, the prince was also eager to help, even when he was unable. Again, his enthusiasm would be more of a hindrance than a help, since his tinkering with the Sooman ship's anti-matter device would eventually lead to the creation of a time warp.<ref name="d4" />

==Behind the scenes==
Plooz made his first appearance in [[David Manak]]'s crossover between the [[Star Wars: Droids (1986)|''Star Wars: Droids'']] and the [[Star Wars: Ewoks (1985)|''Ewoks'']] comic series, which encompassed issues [[Droids (1986) 4|''Droids'' (1986) 4]] and [[Ewoks (1985) 10|''Ewoks'' 10]]. The two issues revolve around Plooz and his various exploits in both time frames.
